expectations.text: business-impact-stated: The draft quantifies or clearly describes the business impact (12% of users, onboarding blocked, etc.)
expectations.evidence: 'roughly 12% of users are unable to complete onboarding... onboarding failure at this scale typically represents meaningful revenue impact and elevated churn risk'

expectations.text: prioritization-framed-as-request: The P1/P2 disagreement is framed as a question for Priya to weigh in on
expectations.evidence: 'Is P2 the right priority given the user and business impact? If it is, I'd love to understand the reasoning so I can communicate it to stakeholders.' — explicitly asks for Priya's input with an off-ramp for engineering.

expectations.text: not-accusatory-toward-thomas: The draft avoids making Thomas look negligent
expectations.evidence: 'Thomas has it on his radar and is working through it' — positive framing. 'I may be missing technical context that informed the P2 call' — explicitly gives engineering benefit of the doubt.

expectations.text: actionable-ask: The draft ends with a clear ask
expectations.evidence: Three numbered, specific questions: priority check, ETA/check-in time, and PM-side unblocking. Highly actionable.
